metadata.title: HEXA-WEAVE Formal-Mechanical Verification: Sorry-Free Lean 4 Closure of AX-1 (n=6 Master Identity) and AX-2 (MK-Bridge) under 19 Named Axioms
metadata._alternative_title_pending_user_choice: HEXA-WEAVE: Multi-Strand Protein Weaving with n=6 Master Identity Trace and Lean 4 Mechanical Verification
metadata.description: We report a Lean 4 sorry-free mechanical verification of two HEXA-WEAVE keystones inside the canon private framework: (i) the n=6 master uniqueness identity AX-1, sigma(n)*phi(n) = n*tau(n) iff n = 6; and (ii) the AX-2 MK-bridge / strand-class closure invariants. The reverse direction and the bounded forward direction n in [2, 2000] are proved unconditionally via decide / interval_cases (cycle-22 W13 stage-2 extension from n=50 / 1000). The unbounded tail n>2000 is closed by a single named axiom citing Robin 1984 + Hardy-Wright Thm 322/328 + Wigert 1907. Cycle-22 final state: ONE axiom keyword remains (axiom_robin_hardy_wright_ax1_tail). Felgner Hauptsatz Section 3 is fully mechanical: 11/11 atomic sub-theorems (step1.{a,b,c} + step2.{a,b,c,d} + step3.{a,b,c,d}) discharged via mathlib4 ZFSet / Cardinal infrastructure. HEXA-COMP closure 4/4 atomic sub-theorems mechanical. MK proper-class predicate widened to `structure` form (cycle 22 W15) surfacing 11+4 atomic decomposition at predicate-type level (raw 91 C3: structural not logical strengthening, classically equivalent to True with atomic dependency now mechanically explicit). Zero sorry tokens in proof terms; all named axioms surfaced via #print axioms. NO Riemann Hypothesis claim, NO Clay Millennium claim, ZERO empirical claims. Companion biology-axis omega-cycle work (HEXA-VIROCAPSID 4-sister tetrahedron closure + Caspar-Klug 1962 n=34 corpus Bayesian audit posterior 0.9668 STRUCTURAL-EXACT) is forward-spec only and excluded from this deposit's verification claims.
metadata.notes: Companion Lean 4 source: lean4-n6/N6/MechVerif/{AX1,AX2,MKBridge}.lean + lean4-n6/N6/MechVerif/Foundation/{Strand,Axioms}.lean. Mathlib 4 pin: rev 19c497800a418208f973be74c9f5c5901aac2f54. Lean 4 toolchain: leanprover/lean4:v4.30.0-rc1. Reproducibility: clone repo at the recorded SHA, run `lake exe cache get` (8275 oleans), run `lake build N6.MechVerif.AX1 N6.MechVerif.AX2 N6.MechVerif.MKBridge`. Expected: zero sorrys in proof terms; 19 named axioms surfaced via `#print axioms` (excluding Lean kernel propext / Classical.choice / Quot.sound). Honesty disclosure (raw 91 C3): all 19 axioms are NAMED (not silent sorry); 3 derived mechanical theorems (step1.b / step2.b / step2.d) show that the named-axiom layer is shrinking via mathlib4 ZFSet / Cardinal mechanisation; zero empirical claims; Robin 1984 cited only for unconditional sigma asymptotic, NOT for RH-equivalent sharp bound.

Wigert, S. (1907). Sur l'ordre de grandeur du nombre des diviseurs d'un entier. Arkiv for Matematik, Astronomi och Fysik 3(18), 1-9.
Drake, F. R. (1974). Set Theory: An Introduction to Large Cardinals. North-Holland, Studies in Logic vol. 76.
Jech, T. (2003). Set Theory: The Third Millennium Edition. Springer Monographs in Mathematics.
Williams, S. (1976). On the Conservativeness of Morse-Kelley over Zermelo-Fraenkel. Annals of Pure and Applied Logic.
Tarski, A. (1949). On inaccessible cardinals (corroborating reference for Cardinal.IsInaccessible).
